name: Haleiwa Ali'i Beach Park
tagline: The inside break at Ali'i is where Haleiwa surf schools launch beginners — small reforms rolling into the harbor with lifeguards watching.
parking: Free lot at the park; fills during winter surf contests but plenty of space otherwise
hours: Open daily, sunrise to sunset; lifeguards on duty
bestTime: Summer for the smallest, most beginner-friendly conditions; the inside reef stays manageable year-round on smaller swell days
talkStory: Haleiwa Ali'i Beach is the competitive face of Haleiwa — the Haleiwa Challenger and other WSL events run here in winter when the outer break produces powerful 15-foot walls. But walk past the contest scaffolding and look at the inside section near the harbor, and you'll find knee-high reforms peeling gently over sand — this is where local surf schools bring their students. The inside break is sheltered from the open ocean energy and rarely exceeds waist height on small days. Restrooms, showers, picnic tables, and a lifeguard tower make it one of the most convenient beginner setups on the North Shore. Just know that when winter swell arrives, the outside break is expert-only territory.
